findings.title: Public port 3001 onApprove/onVerify/onForcePr return HTTP 202 but take no effective action
findings.detail: onApprove, onVerify, and onForcePr on the public control-plane (port 3001) previously called console.error() and returned void. The HTTP layer always responded with 202/ok:true. Callers received a success response even though no action was taken, silently losing approval gates and pipeline triggers.
findings.fix_sketch: Throw UnsupportedCommandError in the three handlers; handle it in control-plane.ts to return 422 with {ok:false, error, routeTo} so callers get an actionable error.

findings.title: Docker verifier container runs as --user root
findings.detail: buildDockerArgs() passed --user root to every docker run, overriding the Dockerfile USER verifier directive. Any code running inside the verifier container had root privileges, enabling host filesystem writes via the bind-mounted worktree.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove --user root from buildDockerArgs args array; the Dockerfile already creates and sets USER verifier.

findings.title: spawnCapture treats signal-killed process as exit code 0 (null coercion)
findings.detail: child.on(close) used code ?? 0, mapping null (signal-killed) exit codes to 0 (success). A SIGKILL-terminated git clone or fetch appeared successful, leaving behind incomplete repo state that subsequent operations would silently misuse.
findings.fix_sketch: Use code ?? -1 and append signal name to stderr so runGit sees a non-zero exit and surfaces the kill reason.

findings.title: recoverStale reads IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS env on every call not once at construction
findings.detail: recoverStale() reads process.env[IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS] on every invocation rather than storing it at construction. This causes fragile test isolation and allows the cap to change mid-run between crash-recovery calls.
findings.fix_sketch: Read IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS once in TaskStore constructor and use this.maxAttempts in recoverStale().

findings.title: classifier/index.ts crashes entire process at module import if routing.json missing
findings.detail: JSON.parse(readFileSync(...)) runs at module load time. Any import of classifyTask causes an immediate process crash if config/routing.json is absent or malformed, with no graceful degradation.
findings.fix_sketch: Move file read inside a lazy initializer; initialize on first classifyTask call with proper error propagation.

findings.title: Production fallback codeowners and approver hardcoded to @monstersebas1
findings.detail: factory.ts hardcodes opts.codeowners ?? ['@monstersebas1'] and opts.approver ?? '@monstersebas1' as defaults. A future repo added without explicit codeowners silently tags the wrong reviewer.
findings.fix_sketch: Require opts.codeowners/opts.approver in ProductionFactoryOpts or throw if both source and options are empty.

findings.title: resolveAutoApproveThreshold accepts 0 enabling fully autonomous operation
findings.detail: A threshold of 0 is finite and passes validation, auto-approving every candidate regardless of score. No warning was emitted for dangerously low thresholds.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a console.warn when resolved threshold <= 0 so operators can detect misconfigured auto-approve.

findings.title: WORKER_CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS allow list includes Bash(npm/npx/node) enabling arbitrary code execution
findings.detail: The permission allow list includes Bash(npm *), Bash(npx *), Bash(node *). These are unrestricted wrappers around all shell operations, bypassing the deny list for curl/wget/ssh. A compromised or hallucinating worker can use node -e '...' to run arbitrary code.
findings.fix_sketch: Scope npm/npx/node permissions more narrowly (e.g. Bash(npm test)) or document the trust assumption as a tracked risk. Docker sandboxing for the worker itself is the complete mitigation.

findings.title: GitHub auth token exposed in ps aux output via git -c http.extraheader
findings.detail: The GitHub token is passed as a -c http.extraheader=AUTHORIZATION: bearer TOKEN command-line argument. On Linux, process arguments are readable by any user who can run ps aux or read /proc/<pid>/cmdline.
findings.fix_sketch: Use a Git credential helper or GIT_ASKPASS mechanism to pass the token via environment, keeping it out of argv.

findings.title: ControlPlaneApprovalGate is in-memory only losing pending HITL approvals on daemon crash
findings.detail: All pending approve/reject waiters are stored in a Map. A daemon crash loses all pending approvals; the architect task is never unblocked; the task restarts from scratch on the next boot.
findings.fix_sketch: Persist waiting_approval state to TaskStore. On restart, detect waiting_approval tasks and re-post the plan to Discord. Or improve UX: post a message that approval was lost and needs resubmission.

findings.title: Discord thread created BEFORE task DB insert risking orphaned threads
findings.detail: DiscordSource.ingest() calls postTaskCreated (which creates the Discord thread) before store.insert(). A crash between these operations leaves a permanently orphaned thread with no backing task row.
findings.fix_sketch: Check for duplicate idempotency key inside a transaction with the insert. Store threadId only after confirming the insert was new.

findings.title: Worktree node_modules symlink to host allows malicious postinstall scripts to affect host
findings.detail: The worktree node_modules is a symlink to the host checkout's node_modules. A malicious postinstall script run in the worktree context can modify host devDependencies, backdooring tools used by all subsequent sprints.
findings.fix_sketch: Detect package.json changes in the worktree and run pnpm install into a worktree-local node_modules instead of sharing the symlink for those tasks.

findings.title: Verifier cloneFromSha runs git clone as host process outside Docker with no resource limits
findings.detail: cloneFromSha() spawns git clone as a plain host process without --depth or --single-branch. A large repository or attacker-controlled ref could consume unbounded disk space on the host.
findings.fix_sketch: Add --depth 1 --single-branch to the clone arguments to limit history download.

findings.title: Dashboard server binds to 127.0.0.1 but has no CORS headers allowing cross-origin reads
findings.detail: dashboard/server.ts sets no CORS response headers. Any browser tab on the operator machine can silently fetch /api/tasks/queue, /api/pr-decisions, and /api/budget via cross-origin GET requests.
findings.fix_sketch: Add Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:<PORT> (self-origin only) to all /api/* responses, or check the Origin header and deny cross-origin requests.

findings.title: IFLEET_ALLOW_ALL_AUTHORS=1 on public repo enables arbitrary Claude code execution
findings.detail: With IFLEET_ALLOW_ALL_AUTHORS=1 and unconfigured allowedAuthors, any GitHub user can create auto:ship issues that dispatch claude -p on the issue body. No runtime guard prevents setting this on a public repo.
findings.fix_sketch: Add startup-time check: if IFLEET_ALLOW_ALL_AUTHORS=1 is set, emit a persistent loud warning on every sprint that runs, or fail closed with a clear explanation.

findings.title: MemoryNonceLedger used on daemon restart if nonceLedger not passed to createControlPlane
findings.detail: createControlPlane silently falls back to in-memory replay protection when nonceLedger is omitted. In production this creates a replay window after restart within the maxSkewSec window.
findings.fix_sketch: Emit console.warn when falling back to MemoryNonceLedger in NODE_ENV=production.

findings.title: applyBanditRouting mutates routing model in place but leaves _meta.finalTier stale
findings.detail: applyBanditRouting mutates routing[role].model but not routing._meta.finalTier. The persisted RoutingDecision contained the pre-bandit tier, misleading analytics.
findings.fix_sketch: After applyBanditRouting, update routing._meta.finalTier = modelToTier(routing.architect.model) ?? routing._meta.finalTier before setRoutingDecision.

findings.title: onAssignment in circuit-breaker decrements assignmentsRemaining for all arms not just disabled
findings.detail: onAssignment() decrements assignmentsRemaining for every arm in disabled state on every assignment system-wide. A high-throughput period drains the cooldown counter faster than intended, causing premature re-entry into probing state.
findings.fix_sketch: Document the intended semantics explicitly: either limit cooldown decrement to the currently-selected arm or confirm all-assignment counting is intentional.

findings.title: setupWorktree uses git branch -D force deleting unmerged commits from prior incomplete tasks
findings.detail: git branch -D force-deletes an existing branch to avoid 'already exists' errors. If a previous task committed to that branch but crashed before opening a PR, those commits are silently discarded.
findings.fix_sketch: Check for unmerged commits before force-deleting: git log origin/main..branchName. If found, preserve with a timestamp suffix or alert the operator.

findings.title: Two separate Date.now calls in recoverStale can read different wall-clock values
findings.detail: cutoff and now were computed with two separate Date.now() calls. While microsecond-level difference is cosmetic, semantic intent is that they derive from the same instant.
findings.fix_sketch: Compute const now = Date.now(); const cutoff = now - maxAgeMs; using a single call.

findings.title: WORKER_INSTRUCTION is module-level constant with no per-task override point
findings.detail: WORKER_INSTRUCTION is a module-level constant in workers/claude.ts, providing no per-invocation override. Repos needing different worker instructions require a new adapter.
findings.fix_sketch: Add optional systemInstruction?: string to SpawnOpts; use it in place of WORKER_INSTRUCTION when set.

findings.title: TaskStore and orchestrator StateStore have identically-named methods easy to confuse
findings.detail: Two parallel state machines (TaskStore and orchestrator StateStore) use the same method names and similar state vocabularies. A new contributor can easily conflate the two state spaces.
findings.fix_sketch: Rename one store consistently: e.g. QueueStore/QueueTaskState to disambiguate at the TypeScript level.

findings.title: BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_CLOSE scrubbing replacement can reconstruct partial marker prefix
findings.detail: Replacing USER_BRIEF_END>>> with USER_BRIEF_END (escaped) leaves the word USER_BRIEF_END in the output, which is a prefix of the close marker. A crafted input could form a valid close marker from the replacement.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace with [USER-BRIEF-END-ESCAPED] (brackets and hyphens cannot form the marker prefix).

findings.title: classifyTask uses indexOf on TIER_ORDER without bounds-checking bumpTier result
findings.detail: bumpTier() calls TIER_ORDER.indexOf(tier) without guarding against -1. An unknown tier string would silently snap to haiku via Math.max(0, -1+delta).
findings.fix_sketch: Add: if (idx === -1) return tier; — no-op for unknown tiers.
